original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: World Health Organization and International Committee of the Red Cross
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Basic emergency care: approach to the acutely ill and injured
original_registry_entry.population: Adults and children with acute illness or injury presenting to first-contact emergency care.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: First-contact emergency units and resource-limited emergency-care settings.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for structured trauma history, secondary survey, extremity perfusion, and open-wound assessment; partial for minor outpatient injury follow-up, imaging, closure, antibiotics, and UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: SAMPLE history and emergency disposition
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports signs and symptoms, allergies, medicines, past history, last intake, events or mechanism, examination, interventions, reassessment, and disposition documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Trauma secondary survey
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports inspection for wounds, bruising, swelling, tenderness, deformity, range of movement, neurological findings, severe pain, and other injury signs.

exact_sections.heading: Extremity trauma and perfusion
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports deformity, distal pulse, capillary refill, sensation, neurovascular compromise, open fracture, splinting, and perfusion reassessment documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Open wound and laceration assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports laceration or abrasion, location, size and depth, bleeding, contamination, foreign-body concern, underlying structures, distal perfusion, dressing, splinting, tetanus, and return or disposition documentation.

title: Basic emergency care: approach to the acutely ill and injured
publisher: World Health Organization and International Committee of the Red Cross
population: Adults and children with acute illness or injury presenting to first-contact emergency care.
intended_setting: First-contact emergency units and resource-limited emergency-care settings.
access_limitations: Algorithm or figure references preserved as locators; diagram structure not interpreted
